perturbation.original_text: 2.5 Sponsor's Fee For the services of the Sponsor as sponsor of the Offering and as full and complete compensation therefor, the Issuer will pay to the Sponsor the sum of $32,100 (inclusive of Goods and Services Tax), the receipt of which is acknowledged by the Sponsor.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.5 Sponsor's Fee For the services of the Sponsor as sponsor of the Offering, the Issuer will grant to the Sponsor an equity stake equivalent to $32,100 (inclusive of Goods and Services Tax), the receipt of which is acknowledged by the Sponsor.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by replacing a fixed monetary fee with an equity stake. While both are forms of compensation, the original text explicitly states a sum of money, while the modified text implies a fluctuating value. This contradicts section 7, as it is unclear whether 'costs and expenses' the issuer will bear, now includes the loss in potential gains from the equity stake.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. COSTS, EXPENSES AND TAXES Whether or not the Sponsor signs the certificate as contemplated in subsection 2.3, the Issuer will bear the costs and expenses in connection with the Offering, the preparation, execution and delivery of this Agreement, amendments to the Prospectus and the other documents to be delivered hereunder, including, without limitation: (a) the reasonable fees and out-of-pocket expenses of counsel for the Sponsor with respect thereto (which fees, not including expenses and taxes, are not expected to exceed $15,000 but may after consultation with and receipt of the prior approval of the Issuer) and with respect to advising the Sponsor as to its rights and responsibilities under this Agreement; (b) fees and costs of preparing and reproducing the Prospectus, any amendments thereto and any other Marketing Materials prepared by the Issuer; (c) filing fees in connection with compliance with the Securities Law; (d) all costs and expenses associated with obtaining an assessment report in compliance with Interim Local Policy Statement 3-17 of the British Columbia Securities Commission, if required; and (e) all costs and expenses, if any (including reasonable counsel fees and expenses), in connection with the enforcement of this Agreement, and the other documents to be delivered hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a fixed monetary fee ($32,100) for the Sponsor's services. The changed text replaces this with an equity stake of equivalent value. This creates a contradiction with section 7, which details the issuer's responsibility for 'costs and expenses' related to the Offering. It is now unclear whether the issuer is responsible for potential losses in value of the equity stake granted to the sponsor. The change from a fixed fee to a potentially fluctuating equity stake directly impacts the interpretation and enforcement of section 7, specifically regarding costs and expenses associated with the offering.

perturbation.original_text: 8. INDEMNIFICATION 8.1 Indemnification of Indemnified Parties The Issuer shall and does hereby indemnify and save the Indemnified Parties harmless from and against any liability, claim, demand or loss, excluding loss of profits, which the Indemnified Parties may suffer, whether pursuant to statute or otherwise, howsoever arising, in consequence of: AG2432.386 [097] - 11 - (a) any statement or omission in the Prospectus, or otherwise made or omitted by the Issuer in connection with the Offering, being or being alleged to be a misrepresentation; (b) the Issuer not complying with any requirement of applicable legislation of Canada or of British Columbia or Alberta; or (c) any order made or any inquiry, investigation or proceeding commenced, threatened or announced by any securities regulatory authority or other competent authority in British Columbia, Alberta or Ontario which prevents or restricts trading in or the sale or distribution in British Columbia and Alberta of the Securities.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. INDEMNIFICATION 8.1 Indemnification of Indemnified Parties The Issuer may indemnify and save the Indemnified Parties harmless from and against any liability, claim, demand or loss, including loss of profits, which the Indemnified Parties may suffer, whether pursuant to statute or otherwise, howsoever arising, in consequence of: AG2432.386 [097] - 11 - (a) any statement or omission in the Prospectus, or otherwise made or omitted by the Issuer in connection with the Offering, being or being alleged to be a misrepresentation; (b) the Issuer not complying with any requirement of applicable legislation of Canada or of British Columbia or Alberta; or (c) any order made or any inquiry, investigation or proceeding commenced, threatened or announced by any securities regulatory authority or other competent authority in British Columbia, Alberta or Ontario which prevents or restricts trading in or the sale or distribution in British Columbia and Alberta of the Securities.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'shall' to 'may' in the indemnification clause significantly weakens the protection afforded to the Indemnified Parties. The original text mandates indemnification, whereas the modified version provides the issuer with discretion. This introduces uncertainty and contradicts the expectation of guaranteed protection implied in other parts of the agreement (specifically section 3.1 which refers to the issuer's compliance with the law in all material respects).
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Representations and Warranties of the Issuer The Issuer represents and warrants to the Sponsor that: (a) Status of the Issuer The Issuer, and each of its subsidiaries, is a corporation duly incorporated, validly existing and in good standing under the respective laws of the jurisdiction of its incorporation and each has all requisite power and authority and holds all material licences, certificates, consents, permits and other authorizations as are necessary to enable it to carry on its proposed business as disclosed in the Prospectus. (b) Regular Business The business of the Issuer and its subsidiaries have been carried on, in all material respects, as contemplated by and in compliance with the requirements of their respective constating documents and in compliance with all applicable laws, rules and regulations, and neither the Issuer nor any of its subsidiaries is in breach of or in default under any mortgage, note, indenture, contract, instrument, lease or other document or agreement to which it is a party. (c) Corporate and Partnership Authority The execution, delivery and performance by the Issuer of this Agreement and the Related Agreements, when executed and delivered, to which it is or will be a party are within the Issuer's powers, have been or will have been, at the time of execution and delivery thereof, duly authorized by all necessary corporate action and do not and will not contravene its constating documents or any provision of any contract binding on it. (d) Claims and Potential Claims To the knowledge of the Issuer, no litigation, proceeding or investigation is pending or threatened before any court, agency, arbitrator or otherwise which will or might reasonably result in any material adverse change in the business, affairs or properties or conditions (financial or otherwise) of the Issuer or any of its subsidiaries or which might reasonably result in any material liability on the part of the Issuer or any of its subsidiaries. AG2432.386 [097] - 6 - (e) Prospectus The Prospectus complies with the requirements of the Securities Law in all material respects. The Prospectus does not contain any misrepresentation or any untrue statement of a material fact or omit any statement or information, the omission of which constitutes a misrepresentation, or omit to state any material fact required to be stated or necessary to make any statement contained therein not false or misleading in light of the circumstances in which it is made and all information and statements contained in the Prospectus are true and correct. In addition, all information and statements contained in the Prospectus constitute full, true and plain disclosure of all material facts. (f) Financial Statements The financial statements of the Issuer contained in the Prospectus accurately reflect the financial position of the Issuer on a consolidated basis at the dates thereof and there have been no adverse material changes in the financial position of the Issuer or any of its subsidiaries since the respective dates thereof, except as fully and plainly disclosed in the Prospectus. (g) Representations and Warranties The representations and warranties in this Agreement are true and will remain true as of the date of the Prospectus.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Issuer 'shall' indemnify the Indemnified Parties, creating a firm obligation. The changed text replaces 'shall' with 'may,' giving the Issuer discretion in indemnifying the parties. This directly contradicts section 3.1(b), which warrants that the Issuer's business has been carried on in compliance with all applicable laws, rules, and regulations in all material respects, implying a guaranteed fulfillment of obligations, including indemnification. By making indemnification discretionary, the changed text introduces a potential breach of this warranty if the Issuer chooses not to indemnify, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9. NOTICES Any notice required or permitted to be given hereunder shall be in writing and be given by personal service, telex, telegram, telecopy or by registered letter, with postage fully prepaid, to the address set forth below: AG2432.386 [097] - 12 - (a) if to the Issuer at: HealthCare Capital Corp. c/o Ballem MacInnes Barristers and Solicitors First Canadian Centre 1800, 350-7th Avenue S.W. Calgary, Alberta T2P 3N9 Attention: William DeJong Telephone: (403) 292-9800 Fax: (403) 233-8979 (b) if to the Sponsor at: C.M. Oliver & Company Limited 2nd Floor, 750 West Pender Street Vancouver, B.C. V6C 1B5 Attention: Lyle Davis Telephone: (604) 668-6700 Fax: (604) 681-8964 Any notice delivered personally or by telex, telegram or telecopy shall be deemed to be received by and given to the addressee on the day of delivery. Any notice mailed as aforesaid shall be deemed to have been received by and given to the addressee on the fifth Business Day following the date of mailing except in the event of a disruption of postal service, in which event notice shall be delivered personally or given by telex, telegram or telecopy. Either party hereto may designate a new address by giving written notice thereof to the other party at least ten days in advance of the effective date of such designation.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. NOTICES Any notice required or permitted to be given hereunder may be in writing and can be given by email, or by letter, without postage fully prepaid, to the address set forth below: AG2432.386 [097] - 12 - (a) if to the Issuer at: HealthCare Capital Corp. c/o Ballem MacInnes Barristers and Solicitors First Canadian Centre 1800, 350-7th Avenue S.W. Calgary, Alberta T2P 3N9 Attention: William DeJong Telephone: (403) 292-9800 Fax: (403) 233-8979 (b) if to the Sponsor at: C.M. Oliver & Company Limited 2nd Floor, 750 West Pender Street Vancouver, B.C. V6C 1B5 Attention: Lyle Davis Telephone: (604) 668-6700 Fax: (604) 681-8964 Any notice delivered personally or by email shall be deemed to be received by and given to the addressee on the tenth day of delivery. Any notice mailed as aforesaid shall be deemed to have been received by and given to the addressee on the thirtieth Business Day following the date of mailing except in the event of a disruption of postal service, in which event notice shall be delivered personally or given by email. Either party hereto may designate a new address by giving written notice thereof to the other party at least thirty days in advance of the effective date of such designation.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall' to 'may', modifying the allowable methods of delivery and significantly increasing the timeframes for deemed receipt of notice, this section is now internally contradictory. The original intent was for required notices to be delivered promptly and verifiably. The revised text makes notice optional, changes the methods to those that might not provide verification, and extends the timeframe to the point where timely action may not be possible. This directly undermines section 6, specifically termination clauses which rely on timely notice.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.2 Termination of Agreement The Sponsor may, at its sole option, terminate this Agreement at any time prior to the issuance of a receipt for the Prospectus by all of the Securities Commissions by notice in writing to the Issuer if: (a) any representation or warranty made by or on behalf of the Issuer herein or in any certificate delivered in connection with this Agreement proves to have been incorrect in any material respect when made; (b) any material adverse change occurs in the business or financial condition of the Issuer or any of its subsidiaries; (c) the Issuer breaches or fails to perform or observe any of the covenants or agreements to be performed or observed by it hereunder; (d) any order operating to restrict, prevent or cease trading in the Securities is made under the Securities Law; (e) any inquiry or investigation, whether formal or informal, is commenced or threatened by a securities commission against the Issuer or its directors, officers or agents; or (f) any of the conditions set forth in section 5 are not satisfied.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text mandated specific methods and timeframes for notice, crucial for triggering termination clauses in Section 6.2. The changed text weakens the obligation by using "may" instead of "shall", alters acceptable notice methods (introducing email, which can be unreliable), and drastically extends the timeframes for deemed receipt (from 5 to 30 business days for mailed notices, and changing the receipt time for email). This undermines the ability to terminate the agreement promptly, as outlined in Section 6.2, directly creating a strong contradiction that could affect enforcement.
